content.claim: Historical ombudsman findings and litigation documented schools unlawfully applying for supplementary ethnic funding while operating segregated classes, and academic literature describes segregated education as incentivized by state funding mechanisms; however, whether identical finance incentives operate under current (post-2020) funding rules remains unestablished.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Portuguese system reform lens
limitations: Sources largely pre-2015; current applicability uncertain
Finance mechanism changes post-2020 not evaluated in retrieved sources
Causal link between funding structure and current SEN identification rates not directly quantified
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
